sanskrit:
ममैवांशो जीवलोके जीवभूतः सनातनः।
मनःषष्ठानीन्द्रियाणि प्रकृतिस्थानि कर्षति॥
transliteration: mamaivāṃśo jīva-loke jīva-bhūtaḥ sanātanaḥ / manaḥ-ṣaṣṭhānīndriyāṇi prakṛti-sthāni karṣati
literal_meaning: My fragmental parts, the conditioned living beings in the world of life, are eternal; with the six senses including the mind, situated in material nature, they are dragged along.
contextual_meaning: The self is an eternal spark of consciousness, not created but temporarily embodied; its suffering arises because the mind plus five senses form a six-fold leash that pulls it through compulsive craving and aversion.
psychological_state: Feels like being a puppet jerked around by six strings—sight, sound, smell, taste, touch and the non-stop inner narrator—yet dimly sensing there is a deeper, unbroken self underneath the tug-of-war.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media at 2 a.m., each swipe a dopamine spike, mind racing, body exhausted, yet unable to stop—realizing the phone is only five senses plus the mind making six hooks that keep pulling you back.
anchor_text: You are the eternal witness, not the six senses pulling you.
